a_image.alt: Postcard UI style mood board — Skeuomorphic · Postal

sections.a_description: Creamy cardstock, a postage stamp in the corner, faded cancellation marks, and the personal warmth of a handwritten message from somewhere far away — Postcard UI brings the nostalgic charm of physical mail to the screen. Every element evokes the tactile pleasure of finding a postcard in your mailbox, with its slightly rough edges, warm tones, and the intimate feeling of a message written just for you.

Postcard styling appears on travel photography blogs, vacation rental sites, and tourism marketing pages. You'll find it on wedding RSVP platforms, pen-pal community sites, vintage stationery shops, and any project that wants to channel the romance and personal connection of physical correspondence in the digital age.

a_image_cover.alt: Postcard UI style use case illustration
Postcard UI style use case illustration
Postcard UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: A vintage postcard — creamy cardstock, a postage stamp accent, postal cancellation marks, and the warm nostalgia of receiving mail from faraway places. Background: soft beige (#f2ece0). The .inner panel simulates a postcard: warm cream (#fdf8f0) background with a thin border (#d5c8b0), gentle shadow, and a decorative postage stamp element in the upper-right corner via a pseudo-element (small square with a wavy red-and-blue border pattern). Faint diagonal cancellation lines across the top-right. Typography mixes formal and handwritten. Set in Lora for body and labels — a warm, readable serif that feels like printed card text. Caveat for the heading — handwriting that mimics a personal message penned on the back of a postcard. Heading in Caveat at 30px, line-height 1.3, color #3e2c20. Eyebrow in Lora at 11px, weight 600, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #a0785a. Body in Lora at 15px, weight 400, line-height 1.7, color #4a3828. Buttons: primary — vintage red (#c0392b), cream text, 4px border-radius. Secondary: transparent with warm border. Alternatives: Crimson Text, EB Garamond, Merriweather.
